appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of inpatient psychiatric admission (CPT 99221) for our patient with major depressive disorder, single episode, severe (ICD-10 F32.2). The denial cited absence of active suicidal plan and intact self-care. Subsequent emergency-department reassessment on 2026-04-22 documents: (1) Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS) Lifetime: ideation type 5 (active with method, intent, and plan) endorsed within the prior 24 hours; (2) PHQ-9 = 25 (severe); (3) patient presented with a written suicide note and a documented stockpile of zolpidem 30 tablets brought in by family; (4) prior outpatient pharmacotherapy with sertraline 200 mg daily for 10 weeks without response; (5) collateral history from spouse documents 6 kg unintentional weight loss over 6 weeks and 4 days of refusal to eat. Per Anthem's licensed MCG Behavioral Health Care Guidelines for Inpatient Psychiatric Admission, medical necessity is established by acute risk of harm to self with documented inability to maintain safety at a less intensive level of care. LOCUS scoring at intake supports Level 6 (Medically Managed Residential Services). The denial reflects an NQTL more restrictive than the analogous medical-surgical NQTL for inpatient observation following overdose risk; per MHPAEA and Wit v. UBH, mental-health medical-necessity criteria may not be more restrictive than generally-accepted standards. Submitted within the 180-day timely-filing window. Attached: C-SSRS, PHQ-9, ED note, collateral letter, medication trial summary, LOCUS scoring sheet.
citations: MCG Behavioral Health Care Guidelines (BHG): Inpatient Psychiatric Admission
LOCUS (Level of Care Utilization System) — American Association for Community Psychiatry
Wit v. United Behavioral Health — Kennedy Forum Case Page
Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA) — CMS overview

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of intensive outpatient psychiatric services (HCPCS S9480) for our patient with recurrent major depressive disorder, severe (ICD-10 F33.2). The denial cited absent step-therapy from a lower level of care. Submitted documentation establishes the required step-therapy: (1) weekly outpatient psychotherapy (CBT) with a licensed psychologist for 12 sessions between 2026-01-08 and 2026-04-02, with treatment-resistant symptoms documented at PHQ-9 trajectory: 22 → 21 → 19 → 18 → 18 → 17 (less than 50% improvement after 12 weeks); (2) concurrent pharmacotherapy with fluoxetine 60 mg daily for 14 weeks; (3) augmentation with aripiprazole 5 mg added 2026-03-12, no meaningful improvement. The case meets LOCUS Level 3 (Outpatient Services, High Intensity) per dimensional scoring attached. Anthem's CG-BEH guideline on Level of Care for Behavioral Health Services adopts LOCUS for adults when MCG BHG is silent or non-specific. Per APA Practice Guideline for Treatment of Patients With Major Depressive Disorder, treatment-resistant MDD warrants intensified care including IOP for ongoing skill acquisition. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: psychotherapy treatment summary, PHQ-9 trend, medication record, LOCUS scoring sheet.
citations: Anthem CG-BEH: Level of Care for Behavioral Health Services
LOCUS (Level of Care Utilization System) — American Association for Community Psychiatry
APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients With Major Depressive Disorder

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of partial hospitalization (HCPCS H0035) for our patient with bipolar I disorder, current manic episode severe without psychotic features (ICD-10 F31.13). The denial cited absence of severity instrument and overnight safety plan. We submit: (1) Young Mania Rating Scale (YMRS) at admission = 28 (severe mania), reassessed 2026-04-19 at 24, 2026-04-22 at 22; (2) Clinical Global Impression — Severity (CGI-S) = 6; (3) overnight safety plan in collaboration with spouse including removal of car keys, removal of credit cards, daily evening phone check-in with on-call PHP team, evening lurasidone 80 mg with witnessed administration; (4) the patient has weekday transportation reliably arranged; (5) mood-stabilizing pharmacotherapy started 2026-04-15 with lithium 900 mg/day and lurasidone 80 mg/day, with documented serum lithium level 0.7 mEq/L at 5-day check. Per Anthem's licensed MCG Behavioral Health Care Guidelines for Partial Hospitalization Program, severity sufficient to warrant structured daily care, capacity to maintain overnight safety, and a measurable treatment plan are required — all three are documented. Per the APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients With Bipolar Disorder, structured intensive day treatment is appropriate during acute manic episodes when 24-hour supervision is not required. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: YMRS trend, CGI-S, safety plan, medication log.
citations: MCG Behavioral Health Care Guidelines (BHG): Partial Hospitalization Program
APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients With Bipolar Disorder

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS, CPT 90867) for our patient with recurrent major depressive disorder, moderate, treatment-resistant (ICD-10 F33.1). The denial cited only 2 antidepressant trials. Complete medication history corrects this oversight and documents 4 adequate trials across 3 pharmacologic classes during the current episode: (1) sertraline 200 mg daily for 10 weeks 2025-04 through 2025-07, ineffective with PHQ-9 22→20; (2) bupropion XL 450 mg daily for 8 weeks 2025-07 through 2025-09, discontinued for tremor and insomnia; (3) venlafaxine XR 225 mg daily for 9 weeks 2025-09 through 2025-12, ineffective with PHQ-9 21→20; (4) duloxetine 90 mg daily for 8 weeks 2025-12 through 2026-02, partial response then loss. Concurrent: CBT for 16 sessions during the same episode. Per Anthem's Medical Policy on Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation, rTMS is medically necessary after ≥4 adequate antidepressant trials from ≥2 classes plus failed evidence-based psychotherapy — all criteria are now documented. The APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients With Major Depressive Disorder supports rTMS as an evidence-based option for treatment-resistant depression. The omission in the original PA submission was administrative; corrected medication history is attached. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: pharmacy fill records, prescribing notes, CBT progress notes.
citations: Anthem Medical Policy: Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation
APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients With Major Depressive Disorder

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of residential substance use treatment (HCPCS H2036) for our patient with severe alcohol use disorder (ICD-10 F10.20 — note: severity classification corrected to 'moderate-severe' per DSM-5 criteria attached). Per the ASAM Criteria, Fourth Edition (2023) adopted by Anthem per its Clinical UM Guidelines, the patient meets Level 3.5 (Clinically Managed High-Intensity Residential Services) on the following dimensional assessment: Dimension 1 (acute intoxication/withdrawal): CIWA-Ar peak 18 with documented seizure at home 2026-03-11; Dimension 2 (biomedical conditions): hepatic transaminitis with AST 184 and bilirubin 2.4, requiring monitored medical care; Dimension 3 (emotional/behavioral): PHQ-9 = 17 with comorbid MDD; Dimension 4 (readiness): high readiness — patient initiated contact and completed initial intake; Dimension 5 (relapse potential): two prior IOP completions with relapse within 30 days each (2024 and 2025); Dimension 6 (recovery environment): unstable housing with active drinking partner. The original denial improperly applied Level 2.1 step-therapy as if the case were Dimension 5 = low; the documented twice-failed IOP history is exactly the criterion ASAM Level 3.5 requires. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: ASAM 6-dimensional assessment, CIWA-Ar trend, lab results, prior IOP discharge summaries.
citations: ASAM Criteria, Fourth Edition (2023) — overview and access
ASAM Criteria Fourth Edition Assessment Guides
Anthem CG-BEH: Substance Use Disorder Level of Care

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of adaptive behavior treatment (CPT 97155) for our 6-year-old patient with autism spectrum disorder (ICD-10 F84.0). The denial cited absence of comprehensive ABA assessment and treatment plan. We submit a complete CG-BEH-02 compliant package: (1) ADOS-2 administered 2026-02-14 with Module 2 comparison score = 8 (high autism spectrum); (2) DSM-5 ASD diagnosis confirmed by board-certified developmental pediatrician 2026-02-21; (3) comprehensive ABA assessment by BCBA-D dated 2026-03-04 (32 pages, attached) including Vineland-3 Adaptive Behavior Composite = 62, ABLLS-R baseline, preference assessment, functional behavior assessment for two target behaviors (elopement, self-injurious head-banging); (4) individualized treatment plan with 14 measurable behavioral and skill-acquisition objectives, 25 hours per week for 12 weeks, with parent training component; (5) CALOCUS-CASII pediatric service intensity score = 5. Per Anthem Clinical UM Guideline CG-BEH-02 Adaptive Behavior Treatment, all four required components are now present. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: ADOS-2 report, diagnostic evaluation, ABA assessment, treatment plan, CASII scoring.
citations: Anthem Clinical UM Guideline CG-BEH-02: Adaptive Behavior Treatment
CALOCUS-CASII — Unified Child & Adolescent Level of Care/Service Intensity Tool (AACAP/AACP)
AACAP Practice Parameters (American Academy of Child & Adolescent Psychiatry)

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of individual psychotherapy 60 minutes (CPT 90837) for our patient with PTSD (ICD-10 F43.10). The denial cited duplicate therapy across two providers. We submit a documented care-coordination plan: (1) Provider A (Dr. R, PhD psychologist) is delivering Cognitive Processing Therapy weekly for trauma-focused work — manualized 12-session protocol, sessions 4-12 remaining; (2) Provider B (Dr. M, MD psychiatrist) is providing 15-30 minute medication-management visits monthly for sertraline 150 mg and prazosin 6 mg daily titration — not psychotherapy; (3) the two providers have an executed bidirectional release of information dated 2026-02-12 and a monthly care-coordination call documented in both charts. The CPT 90837 service is rendered only by Provider A; Provider B's services are 90833 or 90832 add-on or M0064-class management. Per Anthem's Behavioral Health Reference Guide on outpatient concurrent services, distinct non-overlapping therapeutic objectives with documented care coordination are explicitly permitted. The APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients With PTSD recommends trauma-focused psychotherapy plus pharmacotherapy as first-line. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: release of information, care-coordination notes, CPT-coding clarification letters from both providers.
citations: Anthem Behavioral Health Reference Guide — Outpatient Concurrent Services
APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients With Acute Stress Disorder and Posttraumatic Stress Disorder

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of psychiatric diagnostic evaluation (CPT 90791) for our patient with schizophrenia (ICD-10 F20.9). The denial cited out-of-network provider without prior authorization. We submit: (1) the rendering provider, Dr. K, is in-network as of 2026-03-31 per Anthem's provider directory accessed via the Availity portal (screen-capture confirmation attached); (2) the directory entry was incorrect at the time of the patient's referral and Anthem's directory error appears to have prompted the OON classification; (3) per Anthem's Behavioral Health Reference Guide and applicable state network-adequacy standards, members are entitled to in-network cost-sharing when relying on Anthem's published directory. This is also reinforced by MHPAEA NQTL requirements on network adequacy. (4) If Anthem maintains the OON classification, we request retroactive prior authorization pursuant to the Anthem Clinical UM Guideline CG-ADMIN-01 pre-payment review framework, given the clinical urgency (first-episode psychosis on initial evaluation date). Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: provider-directory screenshots, intake referral note, state network-adequacy citation.
citations: Anthem Behavioral Health Reference Guide — Prior Authorization Requirements
Anthem Clinical UM Guideline CG-ADMIN-01: Pre-Payment Review Medical Necessity Determinations
Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA) — CMS overview

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of electroconvulsive therapy (CPT 90870) for our patient with recurrent major depressive disorder, severe with psychotic features (ICD-10 F33.3). The denial cited absence of pre-anesthesia evaluation, baseline cognitive screen, and adequate medication trials. Updated documentation: (1) pre-anesthesia evaluation completed 2026-04-19 by Dr. L (anesthesiology) with ASA classification III and no contraindications to general anesthesia; (2) baseline MoCA = 24 (administered 2026-04-19); (3) pharmacotherapy failures within the current episode: sertraline 200 mg × 9 weeks (no response, PHQ-9 26→25), venlafaxine XR 300 mg × 8 weeks (no response, PHQ-9 25→24), olanzapine 10 mg + fluoxetine 50 mg combination × 6 weeks (partial then loss, PHQ-9 24→22), augmentation with lithium 600 mg × 4 weeks (no response, PHQ-9 22→22). The patient additionally has psychotic features (auditory hallucinations of derogatory content) and active passive suicidal ideation per C-SSRS. Per Anthem's Medical Policy on Electroconvulsive Therapy and the APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients With Major Depressive Disorder, ECT is a first-line consideration in severe MDD with psychotic features. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: pre-anesthesia eval, MoCA, medication history, C-SSRS.
citations: Anthem Medical Policy: Electroconvulsive Therapy
APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients With Major Depressive Disorder

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of sub-acute medically monitored withdrawal management (HCPCS H0010) for our patient with opioid use disorder (ICD-10 F11.20). The denial recommended ASAM Level 2-WM ambulatory management. Updated documentation supports ASAM Level 3.7-WM: (1) peak COWS = 22 (severe withdrawal) on initial assessment 2026-04-30; (2) prior ambulatory buprenorphine induction attempted 2026-02-04 with precipitated withdrawal due to recent fentanyl use, terminating the induction; (3) co-occurring untreated hepatitis C with elevated AST 156, satisfying ASAM Dimension 2 biomedical complications criterion; (4) ASAM Dimension 6 recovery environment: patient is unhoused with active drug-using partner; (5) Buvidal extended-release buprenorphine planned during inpatient stay to bridge to ongoing MOUD per SAMHSA TIP 63 recommendations. Per ASAM Criteria, Fourth Edition (2023), Level 3.7-WM is indicated when dimensional assessment shows multiple severe biomedical/social complications or documented failure of Level 2-WM. Both criteria are present. Per MHPAEA, withdrawal management criteria may not be more restrictive than the analogous medical-surgical inpatient observation criteria for substance-related medical decompensation. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: COWS trend, prior induction note, lab results, housing assessment, ASAM 6-dim grid.
citations: ASAM Criteria, Fourth Edition (2023) — overview and access
ASAM Criteria Fourth Edition Assessment Guides
Anthem CG-BEH: Substance Use Disorder Level of Care
SAMHSA TIP 63: Medications for Opioid Use Disorder
Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA) — CMS overview

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of continued-stay inpatient psychiatric care, days 6-10 (CPT 99231 daily) for our patient with schizoaffective disorder, depressive type (ICD-10 F25.1). The denial cited improved BPRS scores and recommended PHP transition. Continued-stay justification: (1) BPRS improved from 64 to 52 but remains in the severe range; (2) auditory hallucinations command-type persist with content directing self-harm — C-SSRS at day 5 endorses active ideation with method; (3) the patient was started on clozapine 2026-04-26 with current dose 75 mg and required upward titration to therapeutic 300-450 mg range — clozapine titration requires monitored setting with weekly ANC checks (current ANC 2.1) per FDA REMS; (4) the patient cannot safely titrate as outpatient due to active command hallucinations and absent caregiver supervision. Per MCG Behavioral Health Care Guidelines for inpatient continued-stay, persistent severe symptoms with active safety risk during medication titration constitute continued medical necessity. Per APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients With Schizophrenia, clozapine titration is appropriately undertaken in monitored settings. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: BPRS daily trend, C-SSRS daily reassessment, clozapine REMS attestation, ANC trend.
citations: MCG Behavioral Health Care Guidelines (BHG): Inpatient Psychiatric Admission
APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients With Schizophrenia

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of CALOCUS-CASII-supported residential treatment for our 14-year-old patient with severe major depressive disorder, recurrent (ICD-10 F33.2) and post-traumatic stress disorder (ICD-10 F43.10). The denial relied on LOCUS criteria scored on the patient as if an adult. Per Anthem's adopted CALOCUS-CASII assessment for children and adolescents, the appropriate tool yields a composite of 27 (Level 6 — secure 24-hour services with psychiatric monitoring). Documented elements: (1) two psychiatric hospitalizations in the past 6 months; (2) failed PHP and IOP completion attempts within the past 4 months with documented dropout related to ongoing trauma processing; (3) home environment includes ongoing contact with the documented perpetrator of the index trauma per state child-welfare hold dated 2026-04-02; (4) PHQ-A = 24 (severe adolescent depression); (5) CSSRS endorses ideation with plan within prior 72 hours. AACAP Practice Parameters for the Assessment and Treatment of Children and Adolescents With Posttraumatic Stress Disorder support residential treatment in cases with active perpetrator exposure precluding outpatient trauma-focused therapy. Per MHPAEA, applying an adult LOCUS criterion to an adolescent is an NQTL more restrictive than would apply to comparable pediatric medical-surgical inpatient/residential criteria — see post-Wit regulatory guidance. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: CALOCUS-CASII scoring sheet, PHQ-A, C-SSRS, child-welfare hold documentation, prior PHP/IOP discharge summaries.
citations: CALOCUS-CASII — Unified Child & Adolescent Level of Care/Service Intensity Tool (AACAP/AACP)
AACAP Practice Parameters (American Academy of Child & Adolescent Psychiatry)
